title: Credit-card handling: debt + payment categories
description: Per YNAB: credit overspending is handled differently from cash — it increases debt on the card and must be covered later via the credit card's payment category (not deducted from RTA like cash overspend). Add credit-card payment categories tied to credit accounts; when a categorized purchase is made on a credit account, move the assigned money into the card's payment category. Handle credit overspending (increases debt). Parent: bud-n3n.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merge 6988bbe); see commits 64af8eb / ce8022c

title: Cash overspending rolls to next month's Ready to Assign
description: Per YNAB: cash overspending from the previous month is deducted from the new month's Ready to Assign (the overspent category is zeroed at rollover, not carried negative in-category). The current engine rolls the negative Available forward inside the same category instead. Implement month-rollover semantics: at a new month, cash-account category overspend reduces that month's RTA and the category resets to 0. Distinguish cash vs credit (see credit-card issue). Parent: bud-n3n.
close_reason: YNAB foundation merged to main: RTA guard (400 on over-assign), cash-overspend rollover docks next-month RTA w/ category floored at 0, future-month one-pool RTA. 70 backend + frontend tests pass.

title: RTA guard: can't assign money that doesn't exist
description: Per YNAB: money can only be assigned after it exists in an on-budget account; users can't budget future income before it arrives. Today Ready-to-Assign can go negative with no guard. Enforce that an assign cannot push cumulative RTA below zero (or warn/block), in BudgetEngine.assign and the budget router. Surface remaining RTA in the API/UI. Parent: bud-n3n.
close_reason: YNAB foundation merged to main: RTA guard (400 on over-assign), cash-overspend rollover docks next-month RTA w/ category floored at 0, future-month one-pool RTA. 70 backend + frontend tests pass.

title: Epic: Mirror YNAB zero-based budgeting (give every dollar a job)
description: Bring the budget engine in line with YNAB's 'give every dollar a job' model per the user's ynab_rules.md. Core envelope math already exists (src/budget/engine.py: income->RTA, per-month assign, Available rolls forward, RTA=income-assigned). This epic closes the gaps: RTA guard, cash overspending rollover to next month's RTA, credit-card payment handling, targets, auto-assign, future-month correctness, and default category structure. Source rules: /Users/morganldebusk-lane/Downloads/ynab_rules.md
close_reason: YNAB give-every-dollar-a-job epic complete; all children shipped in PR #8 (foundation + targets/credit/auto-assign/default-categories). bud-vp6 (per-txn credit lot accounting) filed as a separate enhancement.

title: Decide & implement access control before LAN exposure (no auth today)
description: BUD has NO authentication and serves all financial data from local SQLite. Exposing it on a network (even LAN) means any device on that network can read/modify the budget. Decide an approach and implement/document it: (a) LAN-only, never port-forward, rely on network trust; and/or (b) reverse proxy (Caddy/nginx) terminating HTTPS + basic auth in front of the app; and/or (c) app-level auth (larger change). Recommend (b) as the pragmatic option for iPhone-over-LAN. This is the security gate for the epic — should be resolved before binding to 0.0.0.0 in any persistent deployment.
acceptance_criteria: A documented, working access-control posture exists before any non-loopback persistent deployment

title: Auto-Assign options (underfunded, last month, averages)
description: Per YNAB: Auto-Assign uses prior budgeting/spending patterns — underfunded amount, assigned last month, average assigned, average spent. Depends on targets (for underfunded). Add an endpoint + UI to auto-fill assignments for a month from these strategies. Parent: bud-n3n.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merge 6988bbe); see commits 64af8eb / ce8022c

title: Future-month assignment correctness + furthest-month RTA
description: Per YNAB: assigning into future months removes money from the current month's RTA; the most accurate RTA appears in the furthest month with money assigned; no hard limit on months ahead (only available cash). The engine already takes a month param and computes RTA cumulatively — verify assigning into future months behaves correctly, the furthest-month RTA is right, and add tests + UI affordance to view/assign future months. Parent: bud-n3n.
close_reason: YNAB foundation merged to main: RTA guard (400 on over-assign), cash-overspend rollover docks next-month RTA w/ category floored at 0, future-month one-pool RTA. 70 backend + frontend tests pass.

title: Targets: weekly/monthly/yearly/custom, full-repeat vs refill
description: Per YNAB: categories can have targets repeating weekly/monthly/yearly or custom cadences (every few months, 1-2 years). Two modes: full-repeat (set aside full amount each cycle, balances accumulate) vs refill (top up to target, leftover reduces next cycle's need). Targets drive 'underfunded' amounts. Add target storage per category + computation of underfunded/needed-this-month. This is how true (non-monthly) expenses work. Parent: bud-n3n.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merge 6988bbe); see commits 64af8eb / ce8022c

title: Auto-classify (label) each new entry on import
description:
Every NEW transaction entry that enters the system (e.g. via OFX import) should be automatically classified/labeled (categorized) as part of the import flow. Today new entries may come in uncategorized.

Preferred approach: AI-assisted classification. A rules-based classifier (match on merchant/description) is an acceptable fallback or first iteration. Entries that can't be confidently classified should fall back to 'uncategorized' for manual review rather than a forced bad guess.
design: AI-assisted preferred; rules-based acceptable as fallback/v1. Low-confidence -> uncategorized.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main: delete uploads w/ cascade (bud-hwl); AI fallback categorization on import (bud-pco). 62 backend + 129 frontend tests pass.

title: Allow deleting uploads
description: Users need the ability to delete a previously uploaded file (OFX import). Currently uploads can be imported but not removed. Add the ability to delete an upload AND its associated imported transactions/entries from the system (cascade delete — removing an upload removes the transactions that came in from that import). Requires frontend UI (delete control + confirmation) and backend API support.
close_reason: Implemented and merged to main: delete uploads w/ cascade (bud-hwl); AI fallback categorization on import (bud-pco). 62 backend + 129 frontend tests pass.

title: Harden DB file permissions to 0600 (owner-only)
description: The SQLite database (~/.bud/cache.db by default, override via BUD_DB_PATH) is created with mode -rw-r--r-- (0644), making your full financial history world-readable to any other account on the host. Database.__init__ (src/cache/database.py:214) creates db_path.parent with mkdir but does not constrain the DB file mode. Tighten to owner-only (0600), and ideally the parent ~/.bud dir to 0700. Implementation: after ensuring the file exists, os.chmod(self.db_path, 0o600) (and the parent dir to 0o700); set the mode on creation so a fresh DB is never briefly world-readable. Apply to the WAL sidecar files too if practical. Acceptance: a freshly created cache.db is mode 0600; existing DBs are tightened on next open; tests/CI unaffected (BUD_DB_PATH temp dirs still work).
acceptance_criteria: Fresh cache.db is 0600 and ~/.bud is 0700; existing DB tightened on open; no test/CI regressions
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merge 6988bbe); see commits 64af8eb / ce8022c

title: Write self-hosting runbook (service manager, build, backup, iPhone access)
description: Document the end-to-end steps to run BUD persistently on a home/mini server and reach it from an iPhone: build the SPA (npm run build), uv sync, run headless with BUD_NO_BROWSER=1 + BUD_HOST/BUD_PORT, keep it alive across reboots (systemd unit on Linux or launchd plist on macOS), back up the single SQLite file (~/.bud/cache.db, override via BUD_DB_PATH), find the host LAN IP, and open http://<ip>:<port> on the phone. Depends on the BUD_HOST work and the access-control decision.
acceptance_criteria: A reproducible runbook covering service setup, persistence, backup, and iPhone LAN access
close_reason: Runbook deliverable docs/self-hosting.md shipped in PR #8 (ce8022c). Was dep-gated on bud-agl sign-off, but the writing task itself is complete; actual LAN deployment tracked under bud-uwb/bud-agl.

Add a mobile nav (hamburger button in the existing header that opens a drawer/sheet with the same NavLinks, closing on navigation). Reuse the existing nav item list. Acceptance: on a <768px viewport every top-level route is reachable; existing md+ sidebar unchanged; basic test or Playwright check for the mobile menu.
acceptance_criteria: On <768px all top-level routes reachable via a menu; md+ layout unchanged
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merge 6988bbe); see commits 64af8eb / ce8022c

title: Make server bind host configurable (BUD_HOST env var)
description: backend/launch.py hard-codes HOST='127.0.0.1', so 'uv run bud' only accepts loopback connections and is unreachable from other devices. Add a BUD_HOST env var (default '127.0.0.1' to preserve safe local behavior) read the same way as BUD_PORT, and pass it to uvicorn.run(host=...). Document that setting BUD_HOST=0.0.0.0 exposes the app on the LAN. Acceptance: 'BUD_HOST=0.0.0.0 uv run bud' is reachable from another device on the same network; default unchanged; update README env-var list and CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: BUD_HOST defaults to 127.0.0.1; setting 0.0.0.0 makes the app reachable from another LAN device; README + CLAUDE.md updated
close_reason: Shipped in PR #8 (merge 6988bbe); see commits 64af8eb / ce8022c

title: Self-host BUD on home server with iPhone (LAN) access
description: Make BUD reachable from an iPhone on the same network by hosting it on a home/mini server. Blocked today by: (1) launcher hard-codes HOST='127.0.0.1' (backend/launch.py:24) so only loopback connects; (2) SPA nav sidebar is 'hidden md:flex' with no mobile menu (AppShell.tsx:82), so phone-width screens cannot navigate between pages. Because the app has NO auth and stores all financial data in local SQLite (~/.bud/cache.db), network exposure also needs a deliberate access-control decision. This epic groups the work to make LAN self-hosting safe and usable.

title: ensure_local_budget races on concurrent first request (UNIQUE constraint failed: budgets.id)
description: get_db() is lru_cached but functools.lru_cache does not serialize concurrent execution of the wrapped fn on a cache miss, so two simultaneous first requests both construct Database() and both run ensure_local_budget(). The check-then-INSERT across two separate connections is not atomic, so the second INSERT crashes with 'UNIQUE constraint failed: budgets.id' (and category seeding could double-insert). Surfaced by the new parallel Playwright data-flow E2E hitting a fresh BUD_DB_PATH. Fix: serialize seeding with BEGIN IMMEDIATE + make inserts idempotent (INSERT OR IGNORE).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #7: ensure_local_budget now takes the write lock up front (BEGIN IMMEDIATE) so a concurrent second init blocks then sees the seeded rows, plus INSERT OR IGNORE guard. Verified: parallel E2E against a fresh DB no longer logs the UNIQUE constraint error.

title: CORS allowlist misses http://127.0.0.1:5173
description: backend/main.py CORS allow_origins lists only http://localhost:5173. The Vite dev frontend opened via http://127.0.0.1:5173 (common habit / some bind configs) gets /api calls blocked by CORS. Fix: add http://127.0.0.1:5173 to allow_origins or use allow_origin_regex. Found in Phase 0 code review.

title: Transactions account filter should use useAccounts, not derive from loaded page
description: TransactionsPage.deriveAccounts builds the account-filter dropdown from the currently-loaded transactions, so accounts with no rows in the current page/filter are missing and the option list shifts as filters change. The authoritative useAccounts() hook already exists. Flagged in the Phase 3 code review (L2).
